Title: Melaka PH to decide position on state govt after DAP pullout
Article:
AYER KEROH: Melaka Pakatan Harapan will decide its future direction in the state government tomorrow following DAP's decision to quit the Barisan Nasional-led administration.
PH chairman Adly Zahari said the views of all assemblymen and component parties within the coalition would be considered before a final decision is announced.
"They (the four DAP assemblymen) have informed us of their decision (withdrawing support).
"Every party in PH has its own right to act. However, when acting in the name of PH, we will discuss it through consensus," Adly said at a press conference.
He added that while the wishes of each party would not be denied, the coalition's priority remained serving the people.
The Bukit Katil assemblyman said he would continue to support Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh as chief minister for now, noting there was no immediate need to withdraw his backing.
Yesterday, Melaka DAP chief Khoo Poay Tiong announced that the party's assemblymen were withdrawing support for the state government to protest a constitutional amendment allowing the appointment of nominated assemblymen.
The decision saw the DAP representatives relinquish all state government posts.
Prime Minister and federal PH chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim later asked DAP to defer its exit from the state administration.
Adly said that the matter would be discussed tomorrow.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: LRT, MRT disruptions drop to 12 in first five months - Loke
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Disruptions on the LRT and MRT networks have shown a declining trend, falling from 177 incidents in 2022 to 28 last year and 12 in the first five months of this year, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said.
Loke said the Mean Kilometres Between Failures (MKBF) had reached 930,000km across all LRT and MRT lines as of May 31, compared with just 90,000km in 2022.
The improvement represents a more than tenfold increase in reliability over four years.
In 2023, the MKBF stood at 160,000km with 111 disruptions. It rose to 330,000km in 2024 as failures fell to 64, and climbed further to 790,000km in 2025, with 28 incidents recorded.
"The latest MKBF achievement as of May 31, 2026, shows an improving trend in overall performance compared with previous years," Loke said.
He was responding in a written parliamentary reply to Shaharizukirnain Abd Kadir (PN-Setiu), who asked for statistics on LRT and MRT trains that experienced breakdowns over the past five years and the short-, medium- and long-term mitigation plans to address service disruptions.
The minister outlined a three-tier mitigation plan underpinning the recovery.
In the short term, Prasarana through Rapid Rail had focused on immediate operational recovery through emergency response procedures, the deployment of relief trains and feeder buses, the placement of transit officers on board trains, on-site repairs and continuous passenger information updates.
For the medium term, efforts were concentrated on improving system reliability through specialised engineering programmes targeting critical components, improvements to preventive maintenance schedules, staff competency upgrades and a Failure Review Panel to identify root causes and ensure corrective action was taken effectively.
The long-term plan involves broader infrastructure development and upgrades, including the addition of new trains, comprehensive maintenance of trains and critical systems, upgrades to communications, signalling and power systems, the implementation of predictive maintenance, the installation of Platform Automatic Gates and improvements to passenger information systems.
On cable theft, which had caused disruptions on the Kajang and Putrajaya MRT lines, Loke said Prasarana together with MRT Corporation would implement phased infrastructure improvements, including the closure of viaduct portals and cable protection measures to reduce the risk of trespassing and asset damage.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US expands sanctions targeting Iran oil, cryptocurrency sectors
Article:
WASHINGTON: The US on Tuesday expanded its sanctions targeting Iran's oil sector, taking further aim at the network of petroleum shipping magnate Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani, the Treasury Department said.
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the department had also frozen US$130 million held in digital wallets linked to Iran's central bank, hitting a sector that has seen increased activity since the start of the war.
The move came after US forces carried out a fourth straight day of strikes against Iran and reimposed a naval blockade, with Iran in turn hitting ships in the Strait of Hormuz, according to the International Maritime Organization.
Iran started blocking the strait – a key waterway for energy transit – after US-Israel attacks in February. Washington imposed an initial blockade on Teheran's ports from mid-April to mid-June.
"This action is part of Treasury's ongoing efforts to ramp up economic pressure on the Iranian regime after it resumed destabilising attacks in the Strait of Hormuz," the Treasury Department said in a notice Tuesday.
It charged that the Shamkhani network remains a key force behind Iran's oil exports, and has expanded into global commodities trading.
The latest move took aim at more than 50 individuals, entities and vessels that it said enabled Iranian authorities to reap profit.
The Treasury Department added that it has now imposed sanctions on over 200 individuals, entities and vessels operating under Shamkhani's patronage.
Shamkhani is the son of security official Ali Shamkhani, an adviser to Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei.
Both were killed Feb 28, the first day of US-Israeli attacks and the start of the Middle East war.
Bessent said the department "sanctioned multiple wallets tied to the Central Bank of Iran, resulting in the freeze of over US$130 million."
"We will continue to aggressively follow the money and deny the Iranian regime access to the proceeds of its illicit revenue schemes," he said in a post on X.
Experts say digital asset platforms have been used to circumvent sanctions placed on Iran's Revolutionary Guards and as a financial safe haven for civilians hit by soaring inflation.
Iran has largely been cut off from the global financial system due to US and European sanctions in place for years before the war. Cryptocurrency has offered a path for citizens and businesses to transact with the rest of the world.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: King grants audience to Johor MB
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today granted an audience to Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi in Johor Bahru.
According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's official Facebook page, Onn Hafiz briefed His Majesty on several matters, including the Johor government's administration following the recently concluded 16th Johor election, as well as the state's administrative direction and development plans.
"His Majesty also expressed hope that the Johor government, having been entrusted with a new mandate by the people, will discharge its responsibilities with dedication, integrity and commitment in implementing every policy and initiative for the well-being of the people and the continued progress of Johor," the statement said.
In Saturday's election, Barisan Nasional (BN) retained its mandate to govern Johor after winning 48 of the 56 seats in the Johor State Legislative Assembly, enabling it to form the government with a larger majority.
The result saw BN increase its representation by eight seats from the 40 it won in 2022, giving the coalition a two-thirds majority in the assembly.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Forest City to enforce legal rights if Network School breached laws
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: Forest City would enforce its full legal and contractual rights if investigations found Network School had breached any Malaysian law, immigration, licensing, land-use or contractual requirements.
The township's developer, Country Garden Pacificview Sdn Bhd, said it would extend its cooperation to authorities investigating allegations linked to Network School, including issues surrounding an individual's nationality.
It said the company fully supported the government's position that Johor must not be used as a base for ideologies, movements or activities that contravene Malaysian law or undermine the sovereignty of Johor and the country.
It said relevant documents would be provided to facilitate a swift, thorough and accurate investigation.
"Should the authorities establish any legal, immigration, licensing, land-use or contractual breach, Forest City will enforce its full legal and contractual rights," it said in a statement today.
The developer also reiterated its zero-tolerance policy towards breaches of the law, misuse of premises, immigration non-compliance or any activity that could jeopardise public safety, public order or national interests.
It said Forest City remained committed to compliance, transparency and safeguarding community safety, Johor's interests and Malaysia's sovereignty.
On July 14, Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi called for an investigation into claims that
Israelis
, who are barred from entering Malaysia, were part of the Network School community and had allegedly entered the country using second passports.
The Home Ministry subsequently
launched an investigation
into the operations of Network School.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman abandoned as infant not entitled to citizenship by operation of law, says Court of Appeal
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: A woman found abandoned at a bus station when she was about one year old is not entitled to Malaysian citizenship by operation of law, the Court of Appeal ruled recently.
A three-member panel comprising Datuk Azhahari Kamal Ramli, Datuk Faizah Jamaludin and Datuk Hayatul Akmal Abdul Aziz held that the constitutional presumption of citizenship applies only to "a newborn child found exposed" and not to children abandoned after infancy.
Delivering the unanimous judgment, Faizah said although the court sympathised with the woman's circumstances, she was not a newborn when she was found and therefore could not rely on the presumption under Section 19B of Part III of the Second Schedule to the Federal Constitution.
"The difficulty is that Section 19B is confined to a newborn child found exposed.
"On the evidence before the court, the appellant was not a newborn child when she was found.
"The Section 19B presumption therefore does not arise," she said in affirming the High Court's decision delivered on April 7 last year.
According to the judgment, the woman was found at the Mentakab bus station in Pahang on Dec 3, 1997. Medical evidence estimated she was about one year old at the time, while a police report put her age at about one-and-a-half years.
She was later raised by a Malaysian couple who formally adopted her under an adoption order in 2012. Her biological parents remain unknown.
Her first birth certificate listed her citizenship as "Belum Ditentukan" (undetermined), while a post-adoption birth certificate later recorded her as "Bukan Warganegara" (non-citizen).
After her application for citizenship was rejected, the woman sought a court declaration that she was a Malaysian citizen by operation of law.
While acknowledging that Section 19B serves an important protective purpose, the court said a purposive interpretation must remain anchored to the constitutional text.
The court rejected arguments that the provision should be interpreted broadly to include all abandoned young children to prevent statelessness.
"It applies to 'any newborn child found exposed in any place'. The words 'newborn child' form part of the threshold requirement. They cannot be omitted from the provision," she said.
The court said the appellant also failed to provide evidence that either of her biological parents was a Malaysian citizen or permanent resident at the time of her birth.
The panel also rejected the argument that her adoption by Malaysian citizens entitled her to citizenship.
The court said an adoption order regularised the legal relationship between an adopted child and adoptive parents but did not retrospectively make them her parents "at the time of birth" for the purposes of citizenship by operation of law.
"The source of citizenship is the Federal Constitution, not the birth certificate," the court said.
However, the court noted that the woman had lived in Malaysia for substantially her whole life after being raised by Malaysian adoptive parents.
"The dismissal of this appeal does not prevent the appellant from pursuing such other route to citizenship as may be available to her under the Federal Constitution.
"Having regard to her age, her circumstances, and the fact that she lived in Malaysia for substantially her whole life, it remains open to her to apply for citizenship by naturalisation under Article 19 of the Federal Constitution," she added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] 'We'll chase you out,' Anwar warns Israelis at Network School
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said the government would expel any Israeli nationals found to be involved in the Network School, a co-living and co-working community in Forest City, Johor.
He said the matter was being investigated by the relevant authorities.
"The Higher Education Minister will give an answer.
"We will investigate. We don't allow (such activities).
"If there is, then action will be taken. If there are Israeli citizens, we don't allow. They will be chased away," he said at the ERM Foundation's "A Celebration of Life and Peace" event at the Mandarin Oriental hotel here today.
Yesterday, the Home Ministry said
it had launched an investigation
into the operations of Network School following allegations that some individuals had entered Malaysia using second-country passports.
The ministry stressed that the government would not tolerate any individual or organisation found abusing Malaysia's immigration facilities or engaging in activities that contravened the law or threatened national security, public order or the nation's sovereignty.
Earlier, Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the state government would not allow any organisation to use the state as a base to spread ideologies that contravened the law or undermined the sovereignty and interests of Johor and Malaysia.
His remarks came amid concerns over Network School, a co-living and co-working community in Forest City.
He said there were claims that Israelis, who are barred from entering Malaysia, were among the community's members and had allegedly entered the country using second-country passports.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PAC welcomes Felcra governance improvements, says adherence must be tested
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The effectiveness of measures to improve governance at the Federal Land Consolidation and Rehabilitation Authority (Felcra) has yet to be tested, said the Public Accounts Committee (PAC).
The improvements were introduced following recommendations from the Auditor General who flagged serious irregularities and weaknesses in governance in 2025.
PAC chairman Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin said the committee noted the governance improvements made by Felcra, which include requiring an external consultant's viability assessment to be presented to the board of directors before any purchase is made.
The committee also acknowledged the introduction of a 15 to 30-day cooling-off period before agreements are signed. This safeguard is designed to prevent rushed decisions that contributed to previous governance failures.
"However, PAC stresses that compliance with this SOP is critically important, given that its effectiveness has yet to be tested as no new commercial asset acquisition has been carried out," Mas Ermieyati told a press conference in Parliament.
The press conference was held after the PAC released a statement on the action taken regarding its nine recommendations linked to Felcra.
On financial projections, the committee welcomed Felcra's revised return-on-investment calculations for its four troubled estates — Telupid, Dabong, Sungai Rawit 2 and Aring.
However, the PAC cautioned that the cumulative impact of higher operating costs and the need for large-scale replanting had shifted the Telupid estate's break-even point from 2030 to 2040.
Therefore, the PAC said future financial simulations must incorporate break-even analysis as a standard requirement.
The committee also welcomed Felcra's medium and long-term strategies to reduce its 90 per cent dependence on foreign workers, as well as the decision to suspend all new commercial land bank acquisitions to ease its financial burden.
On board governance, the committee welcomed the Finance Ministry's proactive deployment of the GCAIS 2.0 reporting system, which enables ministry representatives on Felcra's board to flag critical issues directly and promptly.
"PAC stresses that absolute compliance with the strengthened new acquisition SOP is critically important to ensure the sustainability of investments, avoid any risk of future losses and safeguard the company's long-term financial stability," Mas Ermieyati said.
In July 2025, Mas Ermieyati said the committee would summon the Rural and Regional Development Ministry and Felcra Bhd to explain the management of its oil palm plantation procurement.
The Auditor General's Report 2/2025 flagged
significant governance failures
in Felcra's RM241.76 million acquisition of four oil palm estates between 2022 and 2024.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Organised crime evolves into sophisticated business, says MCBA chief
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Organised crime has evolved beyond conventional illegal activity into a sophisticated business enterprise driven by technology, global networks and professional expertise.
Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain said modern organised crime syndicates had developed their own ecosystems, enabling them to expand systematically across geographical and national borders.
"One thing that is clear about organised crime is that it is a business activity. Crime is a business entity. They do not have to comply with the concepts of sovereignty, democratic procedures or government political institutions. They have their own simple rules which they follow among themselves.
"In the past, a criminal had to cross borders to commit crimes in another country. Today, they can attack victims in one country, launder money through two others, use infrastructure hosted in four more countries and disappear before sunrise. Nothing moves except information," he said in his keynote address at the 17th Asian Criminological Society (ACS) Conference 2026 here today.
Speaking at the conference themed Contemporary Trends and Patterns of Crime: Criminological Impacts, Shuhaily said crime had become borderless, less physical, anonymous and enjoyed broader access to financial systems without geographical constraints.
He said the key issue was not technology itself, but that criminal activities were no longer confined by physical boundaries.
Shuhaily said scam centres had emerged as increasingly powerful criminal entities, fuelled by cybercrime such as phishing, malware and artificial intelligence-powered voice cloning, which had become easier to deploy.
He said criminal groups adapted to technological changes faster than governments because they were not constrained by administrative procedures or lengthy procurement processes.
"For criminals, their market is now bigger than ever. Smuggling contraband, drugs, weapons, prohibited goods and human trafficking have become much easier through anonymous transactions on the dark web, making these activities highly profitable with relatively low risk.
"These activities pose greater challenges for countries like Malaysia, with extensive maritime borders and hundreds of illegal entry routes. For organised crime syndicates, however, there is only one language they understand - maximise profits," he said.
Shuhaily said criminal syndicates were also increasingly outsourcing illicit operations by hiring companies specialising in voice cloning and programming services.
"Ironically, some of those carrying out these jobs may not even realise they are facilitating criminal activities because they view the work as legitimate service-based employment.
"When financiers offer technical opportunities to fresh graduates, unemployed individuals or experienced technicians, such jobs can become attractive alternatives, particularly in countries facing high unemployment or economic crises," he said.
He said the nature of crime, offenders and victims had all changed, raising questions over whether technology had evolved quickly enough to keep pace with emerging criminal threats.
"Crime has evolved, criminals have changed and victims have also changed. The question before us now is whether technology has changed or been adapted quickly enough to understand this new form of crime," he said.
To address the evolving threat, Shuhaily said national security technology and criminology could no longer remain static, proposing three key shifts in approach.
First, he said criminologists should move beyond merely understanding crime to developing stronger predictive capabilities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCBA: Inspection dispute leads to shooting threat against officer
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Dissatisfaction over an inspection process is believed to have prompted an individual to threaten to shoot a Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) officer.
MCBA director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain said preliminary checks found that the incident occurred after the suspect, believed to be an agent, allegedly failed to cooperate with officers during the initial stage of an inspection at Port Klang.
He said the MCBA officer was merely carrying out his duties in accordance with standard operating procedures before a misunderstanding arose with the suspect.
"Based on our initial checks, the suspect was uncooperative at the early stage. Our officer was simply carrying out his duties and there was a slight misunderstanding," he said after attending the 17th Asian Criminological Society (ACS) Conference here today.
It was reported yesterday that a man, believed to be a shipping agent, allegedly threatened to shoot an MCBA enforcement officer while the officer was carrying out official duties at Port Klang.
Port Klang MCBA commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said a police report had been
lodged
at the Klang Selatan district police headquarters following the incident.
Shuhaily said the suspect openly threatened the officer and the incident was witnessed by several people at the scene.
"Following the incident, MCBA immediately lodged a police report to enable investigations to be carried out and appropriate action to be taken against the suspect," he added.
He declined to comment further on possible police action, saying the investigation was now under the jurisdiction of the police.
"With the suspect having been identified, there may be an arrest by the police, but I cannot comment on their actions.
"The investigation is being conducted by the police and any further queries should be directed to them," he said.
On whether MCBA officers should be equipped with firearms following the incident, Mohd Shuhaily said the move should be expedited.
"It should be, and I believe the ministry is working towards procuring firearms. That is all I can comment on," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heritage conservation needs corporate support, says minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Companies should do their part in heritage conservation and development, National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang said, urging more public-private collaborations.
He said heritage development should not rely solely on government funding, citing sustainable models used in other countries.
"As suggested by the second finance minister (Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan), the public-private partnership approach must be strengthened to ensure that heritage conservation and development do not depend entirely on government funds.
"In developed nations like the United Kingdom, corporate partnership models with museums are well-established, such as the Samsung Digital Discovery Centre, which is a collaboration between Samsung and the British Museum," Aaron said during the ministry's monthly assembly at the National Museum today.
He said the RM5 million Museum Matching Grant should be leveraged to expand these partnerships.
Museums and heritage institutions, he said, should work with industry players to design more creative, relevant and engaging exhibitions that showcase the nation's cultural and historical assets.
"I hope this grant will be fully utilised to build broader strategic collaborations between museum institutions, government-linked companies (GLCs), the private sector and the corporate community," he said.
Aaron also urged the Museums Department to strengthen its promotional and communication strategies with fresh, creative and accessible content that resonates with the younger generation.
"Embracing digital technology is no longer an option but a necessity in today's landscape, particularly for museum digitalisation," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aaron wants national database to link museum artefacts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang has urged the Department of Museums Malaysia to establish a National Artefact Collection Database to consolidate information from museums nationwide.
He said Malaysia should emulate countries such as Norway and Sweden, which connect hundreds of museums through digitised systems for public access.
"I propose that the ministry's secretary-general and the director-general of the Department of Museums Malaysia hold engagement sessions with all 232 museums under federal agencies, state governments, universities and the private sector to realise this initiative.
"For example, Norway and Sweden developed DigitaltMuseum, which links hundreds of museums and has digitised millions of collection items for research, education and public access," Aaron said during the ministry's monthly assembly at the National Museum today.
He added that Malaysia is also set to make museum exhibits more gies for selected historical topics.
Director-general of the Department of Museums Malaysia Kamarul Baharin A. Kasim said the first immersive exhibition would be housed at Gallery D of the National Museum in Kuala Lumpur.
"For the independence declaration display, we want to capture the atmosphere and ambience so visitors feel as if they are standing in Stadium Merdeka during that historic moment.
"Another immersive project funded by a matching grant is at the Sungai Lembing Museum, recreating the underground tin mining experience. We expect the project to be completed by August," Kamarul told reporters.
Aaron said a virtual exhibition platform would also be developed for the Lembah Bujang Archaeological Museum to showcase its artefacts and historical heritage to the public.
Meanwhile, the department recently received 109 artefacts donated by the public through a donation campaign, bringing the total number of items under its custody to 437,242.
As of May 2026, museums under the department recorded 766,939 visitors, with the 2026 National Unity Week celebration attracting 284,448 visitors – the highest attendance since the event was first held in 2023.
Between 2023 and 2025, the department also coordinated 41 temporary exhibitions.
"I have requested the director-general to present upcoming exhibition plans for this year and next year during the next post-cabinet meeting," the minister said.
During the assembly, Aaron also launched the National Unity Ministry Strategic Plan 2026–2030, aimed at strengthening national values, social integration and heritage preservation.
A Policy Impact Rubric was introduced as a new monitoring tool to track progress and evaluate the impact of each unity initiative.
Operating alongside the primary roadmap, the ministry's Digitalisation Strategic Plan 2026–2030 outlines a digital transformation agenda centred on three main priorities: empowering integrated digital service delivery, securing modern digital infrastructure, and strengthening digital governance and ICT capabilities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's Q2 GDP growth expected to moderate: economists
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's second quarter (2Q 2026) gross domestic product (GDP) growth is expected to moderate to 4.8-5.0 per cent from 5.4 per cent in 1Q 2026.
IPP Financial Advisers (IPPFA) director of investment strategy and country economist, Mohd Sedek Jantan said Malaysia's domestic economy remains resilient, despite heightened geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and renewed volatility in global energy prices.
"We believe stronger domestic demand, resilient external trade and a supportive policy environment more than offset external headwinds, allowing economic activity to remain on a solid expansion path," he said in a statement today.
Meanwhile, Mohd Sedek said that although manufacturing activity softened during parts of the quarter, business conditions improved progressively, with the manufacturing purchasing managers index (PMI) recovering from 49.9 in May to 50.5 in June, signalling a return to expansion.
He said this suggests that manufacturers had successfully navigated supply chain disruptions and external uncertainties while continuing to benefit from the global artificial intelligence-driven technology upcycle and sustained demand for electrical and electronic exports.
Domestic demand also remained as the principal engine of growth, with wholesale and retail trade at RM174 billion in April and RM171 billion in May – among the strongest readings since the observation period began in 2024.
This reflects resilient household consumption despite elevated living costs, he added.
"Macroeconomic fundamentals remained supportive throughout the quarter, with inflation staying well contained despite higher global crude oil prices, largely due to the government's decision to maintain the RON95 petrol subsidy at RM1.99 per litre, limiting pass-through to transportation and consumer prices.
"At the same time, Bank Negara Malaysia's stable and predictable overnight policy rate continued to support credit growth, business investment and household spending without creating excessive inflationary pressures," he said.
Mohd Sedek said the Malaysian economy appears to be increasingly supported by stronger internal demand and policy stability.
He said the combination of resilient consumption, improving investment sentiment, robust exports and contained inflation suggests that growth remained broad-based rather than concentrated in a single sector.
"Consequently, we expect Malaysia's economy to maintain a healthy momentum in 2Q 2026, reinforcing our confidence that the country remains on track to achieve our 2026 full-year GDP growth forecast of 4.6 per cent.
"Despite that, geopolitical developments and global trade conditions will continue to warrant close monitoring," he said.
Malaysia's 2Q 2026 GDP advance estimates are scheduled to be released by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) this Friday (July 17).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Get a physical driving licence if you're driving to Thailand, RTD urges public
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Road Transport Department (RTD) has urged Malaysians driving into Thailand to obtain a physical driving licence to avoid being fined by Thai authorities.
Kelantan RTD director Mohd Misuari Abdullah said digital licences were sufficient for driving in Malaysia, but physical licences were mandatory in Thailand.
"Apart from physical licences, those travelling abroad can also visit any RTD counter to obtain an International Driving Permit (IDP)," he said on the sidelines of the Kucha Sugho Queen Ekspress programme.
He said a physical licence costs RM20.
Misuari's comments follow reports of a Malaysian driver being fined 1,000 baht, or RM121, during a roadblock in Hat Yai, Thailand, for failing to produce a physical licence.
During the inspection, the driver had presented her
digital driving licence
via the MyJPJ application.
However, Thai police said they only recognised physical licences or an IDP.
In February 2023, Transport Minister Anthony Loke announced that Malaysian private vehicle owners would no longer need to display their road tax or carry a physical copy of their
driving licence
, as digital versions of these documents would be available through RTD's website and the MyJPJ app.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MMA wants healthcare workforce dashboard to drive reforms
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Medical Association (MMA) has called for a comprehensive healthcare workforce dashboard to complement the Health Ministry's newly granted autonomy over workforce management, saying it is essential to improve transparency and support evidence-based workforce planning.
Its president, Datuk Dr Thirunavukarasu Rajoo, said greater operational autonomy would enable the ministry to manage, organise and deploy its approved workforce more efficiently based on service needs.
The association also welcomed the offer of permanent appointments to 4,500 contract medical officers, the establishment of the Health Ministry-Public Service Department (PSD) Workforce Management Task Force, closer collaboration with the Higher Education Ministry on long-term workforce planning, and the introduction of relocation benefits for eligible medical officers.
However, he said the reforms would only succeed if they were backed by effective implementation through clear operational guidelines and the consistent application of the revised ePlacement mechanism to ensure eligible medical officers receive their relocation benefits promptly.
"Greater transparency will strengthen public confidence, facilitate better workforce planning and enable more timely interventions to address healthcare workforce challenges," he said in a statement today.
He said healthcare workforce reform should go beyond creating permanent posts and instead focus on deploying the right healthcare professionals to the right places at the right time, supported by clearer career pathways, postgraduate training opportunities and policies that promote long-term retention in the public healthcare system.
Thirunavukarasu also reiterated MMA's call for an independent Health Service Commission as a long-term solution to oversee healthcare workforce planning, career development and human resource management.
The association said it remains committed to working closely with the ministry to ensure the reforms are strengthened, responsive and sustainable.
"While the announcement marks an important milestone, it should also be viewed as the beginning of broader healthcare workforce reforms," he said.
On July 14, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad announced measures to
strengthen and retain
the public healthcare workforce,  including greater autonomy over workforce management, permanent appointments for 4,500 contract medical officers, relocation benefits, and improved pathways to specialisation through master's and parallel pathway programmes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Melaka allocates RM5mil for immediate flood repair works
Article:
MELAKA: The Melaka government has approved an immediate allocation of RM5 million to carry out repair and restoration works on drainage systems and infrastructure damaged by the floods that hit the state on Saturday.
State Senior Housing, Local Government, Drainage, Climate Change and Disaster Management Committee chairman Datuk Rais Yasin said the decision was made during the Post-Flood Coordination Meeting he chaired on Monday.
"The scope of works includes riverbank repairs, repairs to control structures, bridge repairs, maintenance and cleaning of drainage systems, as well as roads damaged by the floods," he said during the State Legislative Assembly sitting at Kompleks Seri Negeri here today.
He was replying to a supplementary question from Allex Seah Shoo Chin (PH-Kesidang), who asked about the immediate measures taken by the state government to address the recent flash floods in several parts of Melaka.
Rais said the state government was also implementing medium-term measures, including maintenance and upgrading works at the Paya Rumput Flood Retention Pond, which has received a RM3 million allocation from the Ministry of Energy Transition and Water Transformation (Petra).
"The works are expected to begin in August, with three main components, including increasing the pond's retention capacity through sediment dredging and cleaning works to accommodate high volumes of rainwater," he said.
Earlier, Datuk Siti Faizah Abdul Azis (BN-Sungai Rambai) asked whether the state government planned to implement flood mitigation projects in Sungai Rambai, which frequently experiences flash floods.
In response, Rais said the Department of Irrigation and Drainage (DID) had approved RM250,000 to appoint consultants to conduct a flood mitigation feasibility study for the Sungai Rambai constituency.
He added that the state government had also taken note of the Sungai Kesang Flood Mitigation Project being implemented by the Johor DID, which is expected to help reduce flood risks for residents in the Sungai Rambai area.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sofia Rini charged with defaming businessman Farhash
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 50-year-old woman pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court today to a charge of defaming businessman Datuk Seri Farhash Wafa Salvador Rizal Mubarak.
Sofia Rini Buyong was accused of making defamatory remarks against Farhash in a video, knowing or having reason to believe that the statements would harm his reputation at 6.30pm on Apr 26.
She entered her plea after the charge was read before magistrate Nurul Izzah Hasan Basri.
"I claim trial," she said calmly, shaking her head.
The charge, framed under Section 500 of the Penal Code, carries a maximum two years' jail, a fine, or both upon conviction.
The prosecution was led by state prosecution director Datuk Nordin Ismail, assisted by deputy public prosecutor Mohamad Fadhly Mohd Zamry.
Fadhly asked the court to set bail at RM15,000 with one surety.
However, defence lawyer Shugan Raman sought a lower bail of RM500, arguing that his client is bankrupt, unemployed and poses no flight risk.
"She is currently on Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission bail of RM10,000, and all her available funds are tied up there.
"The amount proposed by the prosecution is beyond her financial means. It is excessively high and would effectively prevent her from posting bail.
"Bail should not be punitive but should serve only to ensure her attendance in court," he said.
He said she came to court voluntarily today to face the charge and has cooperated fully throughout the investigation.
Shugan also told the court that his client suffers from hypertension and diabetes and is supporting her son.
In reply, Fadhly maintained that the prosecution's proposed bail was reasonable given the nature of the charge.
"If the bail amount is too low, the risk of her absconding would be higher," he said.
After hearing submissions from both parties, Nurul Izzah set bail at RM7,000 with one surety and fixed Aug 28 for mention.
Outside the courtroom, Shugan said he was unsure whether his client's son, who intended to stand as her surety, would be able to raise the money to post bail.
"The amount is still beyond the family's financial means," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ahmad Badri appointed Felda chairman, succeeds Ahmad Shabery
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) has appointed Tan Sri Ahmad Badri Mohd Zahir as its chairman, succeeding Datuk Seri Ahmad Shabery Cheek, whose three-year tenure ended on June 30.
In a statement, Felda said the appointment reflects the government's confidence in Ahmad Badri's leadership, integrity and extensive experience in leading strategic organisations.
His leadership is expected to strengthen governance, enhance management excellence and continue Felda's transformation agenda in line with the aspirations of sustainable and inclusive national development.
Felda said Ahmad Badri has more than three decades of experience in the public and corporate sectors since joining the civil service at the Ministry of Finance in 1989.
It said he had held various strategic positions throughout his career before being appointed as Malaysia's Treasury secretary-general from 2018 to 2020.
"Previously, he was appointed chairman of the Employees Provident Fund and served on the boards of several key national institutions, including Bank Negara Malaysia, Permodalan Nasional Bhd and Tenaga Nasional Bhd.
"He currently serves as chairman of RHB Bank Bhd and chairman of the Malaysian Rubber Board, in addition to being a board member of Sime Darby Bhd," it said.
Academically, Ahmad Badri holds a Master of Business Administration (MBA) from the University of Hull, United Kingdom, and a bachelor's degree in property management from Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM).
Felda said it is confident that under Ahmad Badri's leadership, the organisation will continue to strengthen the transformation agenda of the Felda Group, enhance economic sustainability and create greater value for more than three million settlers.
"His leadership is expected to help Felda achieve new milestones while ensuring the wellbeing of settlers and safeguarding prosperity for future generations," it said.
In a previous statement, Felda said Ahmad Shabery had spearheaded various key initiatives during his tenure to support the organisation's strengthening and transformation agenda.
These include efforts to enhance corporate governance, build strategic partnerships and boost stakeholder confidence in Felda.
Ahmad Shabery was appointed Felda chairman on July 1, 2023.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thai authorities arrest three over civil service exam rigging scandal
Article:
BANGKOK: Thai authorities have arrested three key suspects over a nationwide civil service examination rigging scandal that could cost about 5,000 officials their jobs.
According to the Bangkok Post, Thai Prime Minister and Interior Minister Anutin Charnvirakul confirmed that the three main suspects were in custody as police continued gathering evidence to build the case.
He said the investigation would uncover the full extent of the scheme and that those involved would be brought to justice.
The three suspects are Win Thanaphatcharaphokin, an adviser to the Promotion Office and Training director at Kasetsart University; his younger sister, Sataporn Thanaphatcharaphokin; and Pichit Thangphrom, former director of the Strategy and Budget Division of Wichian Buri Municipality in Phetchabun province.
As interior minister, Anutin oversees the Local Administration Department (DLA), which organised the examinations and is at the centre of the scandal.
Central Investigation Bureau commissioner Police Lieutenant General Nathasak Chaonasai said the trio allegedly enlisted 11 civil servants to alter the examination scores of thousands of candidates at Pichit's residence in Nonthaburi province.
The 11 officials are expected to be charged next week with illegal assembly, document forgery and entering false information into a computer system, the same offences faced by the three principal suspects.
Investigators are also working to identify the person who supplied the examination score files to Pichit.
Interior Ministry permanent secretary Unsit Sampuntharat said investigations had uncovered irregularities involving about 5,000 of the 15,000 local government officials recruited this year, with those implicated likely to be dismissed.
He said everyone found to have been involved in manipulating the examination process would face legal action.
The DLA conducted the examinations late last year, attracting about 400,000 candidates competing for 6,700 local government positions.
Investigators believe thousands of candidates paid bribes ranging from 350,000 baht to 800,000 baht, depending on the position sought, in exchange for passing grades.
Responding to allegations that people linked to the examination fraud had ties to senior members of the Bhumjaithai Party, Anutin said no one would be above the law.
"If it involves anyone, action will be taken against them all," he said.
He added that there would be no exceptions, including for members of his own party, and that anyone found to be involved would face legal action.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court-ordered compensation to road crash victims, family a strong message
Article:
LETTERS:
I welcome the Transport Ministry's proposal to empower the courts to order irresponsible and reckless drivers convicted of serious road traffic offences to pay compensation to victims or, in cases involving fatalities, to their lawful next of kin.
This proposal represents an important step towards strengthening accountability on our roads and ensuring that justice extends beyond criminal punishment alone.
Road crashes often leave victims and their families with enormous physical, emotional and financial burdens.
While imprisonment, fines or licence disqualification serve to punish offenders and deter dangerous behaviour, they do not necessarily address the losses suffered by victims, including medical expenses, rehabilitation costs, loss of income and the long-term impact on families.
A court-ordered compensation mechanism recognises that those whose reckless actions cause serious harm should bear a greater measure of responsibility for the consequences of their conduct. It also reinforces the principle that road safety is not merely a legal obligation but a social responsibility.
At the same time, the implementation of this proposal must be carefully designed. Compensation orders should be made by the courts based on the facts of each case, taking into account the severity of the injuries, the actual losses suffered by the victims and the offender's financial circumstances, so that the process remains fair, proportionate and practical.
The proposed mechanism should complement, rather than replace, existing avenues of compensation such as insurance claims and civil actions. Clear guidelines will also be necessary to avoid duplication and ensure that victims receive timely and meaningful relief.
Ultimately, this proposal sends a strong message that reckless driving has real human consequences. If implemented effectively alongside stronger enforcement, road safety education and improvements to the overall road transport system, it can contribute to a more responsible driving culture and provide greater justice and support for victims and their families.
Every road user has a duty to protect the lives of others. Those who wilfully disregard that duty and cause death or serious injury must be prepared to face not only criminal sanctions, but also the responsibility of compensating those whose lives have been irrevocably affected.
Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye
Road Safety Council member
The views expressed in this article are the author's own and do not necessarily reflect those of the New Straits Times
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] All 266 foreigners in Forest City community hold valid papers, says Immigration
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Preliminary checks by the Immigration Department found that all 266 foreigners inspected at a community in Forest City possessed valid immigration documents, although investigations into the controversial "Network School" are ongoing.
Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the inspection was carried out on July 14 in a joint operation involving the Iskandar Puteri City Council (MBIP), the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), the Johor State Secretary's Office and the police.
"We checked 266 foreigners from 40 countries and found that all of them possessed valid immigration documents based on current records.
"Investigations are being conducted to ensure full compliance with entry conditions, the Immigration Act 1959/63 and other relevant laws," he said in a statement today.
He said the department would also investigate any new information related to identity fraud, misuse of travel documents or immigration facilities, and breaches of pass conditions.
He said the department would not compromise on any breaches of immigration laws, adding that enforcement action would be taken against those found to have violated the law.
"The department will continue working closely with the
Home Ministry
and other relevant agencies to ensure all matters under their respective jurisdictions are thoroughly examined in accordance with the law to safeguard national security, sovereignty and national interests," he said.
Yesterday, the Home Ministry launched an investigation into issues surrounding the operations of
Network School
in Forest City, Johor.
The probe follows allegations that some individuals had entered Malaysia using second-country passports.
The ministry said it viewed seriously any matter involving national security, compliance with immigration laws and the misuse of Malaysia's immigration facilities.
The investigation, involving the Immigration Department, police and other relevant agencies, will focus on verifying the identities and nationalities of those involved.
It will also examine the validity of their travel documents, the type of immigration passes or facilities granted, the purpose of their entry into Malaysia, and whether they had complied with immigration and other relevant laws.
Recently, the Johor government said it would not allow any organisation to use the state as a base to spread ideologies that contravene the law or undermine the sovereignty and interests of Johor and Malaysia.
Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said this when addressing concerns over Network School, a co-living and co-working community in Forest City.
He said there were claims that Israelis, who are barred from entering Malaysia, were among the community's members and had allegedly entered the country using second passports.
He also said the passes and passports, including any second passports held by those linked to the organisation, must be checked.
Last October, Israeli vlogger Nuseir Yassin, better known as Nas Daily, sparked controversy after appearing in a video filmed in Johor promoting Network School in Forest City, despite Malaysia's ban on Israeli passport holders.
The video, which featured Johor landmarks and views of Singapore, has since been removed.
In May, fresh claims surfaced over the presence of Israelis at Network School.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rapid Bus names former Kontena Nasional chief Syahrul Azmir as CEO
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Rapid Bus Sdn Bhd has appointed Syahrul Azmir Ahmad Zaki as its new chief executive officer effective from July 15, 2026.
The company's chairman Datuk Seri Mustafar Ali said that Syahrul's appointment comes at a crucial time as the company continues to strengthen the country's public bus network to meet growing mobility demands.
"Syahrul's extensive experience in leading large-scale operations and driving organisational transformation will further strengthen Rapid Bus' efforts to improve service reliability, enhance customer experience, and accelerate the company's digitalisation and sustainability agenda.
"We are confident he will lead Rapid Bus to greater heights in meeting the needs of commuters while supporting the nation's public transportation aspirations," he said.
Syahrul brings more than 27 years of experience in the logistics, supply chain and transportation sectors, having held leadership positions at Aramex Malaysia, RUAG Aviation Malaysia, Kerry Logistics, OBYU Holding and Kontena Nasional Bhd.
His expertise spans large-scale operations management, asset maintenance, corporate strategy and organisational transformation, with a strong track record of improving operational efficiency and business performance.
Under Syahrul's leadership, Rapid Bus will focus on improving operational performance, strengthening customer experience, developing its workforce and delivering more responsive and reliable services.
The company will also accelerate the deployment of electric buses, advance digital transformation initiatives and strengthen first- and last-mile connectivity to make public transport more accessible and convenient for commuters.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 100 boxes of suspected illicit liquor seized, two men held
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 100 boxes of suspected untaxed liquor were seized and two men, including an Indian national, were detained during the Op Taring Alpha 2 Intra/Inter raid on Jalan Berai, Klang, Selangor, yesterday.
General Operations Force (GOF) Battalion 4 Commanding Officer Superintendent Amanjit Singh said that during the 1pm raid, both suspects, in their 40s, were arrested while transferring boxes from a lorry into a car under suspicious circumstances.
"Upon checking the two vehicles, we found 100 boxes of Royal Dutch brand liquor on which customs duty had not been paid, a lorry, a car and a mobile phone, with the total seizure estimated at RM409,818," he said in a statement today.
He said the raid was carried out following intelligence gathered by personnel from the Intelligence Branch of GOF Battalion 4 in Semenyih, with cooperation from the Criminal Investigation Division and the Crime Prevention Patrol Unit of the Klang Selatan police headquarters.
The suspects and all seized items were taken to the Klang Selatan Police Headquarters for further investigations under Customs Act 1967, Immigration Act 1959/63 and the Road Transport Act 1987.
Amanjit also called on the public to continue to cooperate with the police by providing information regarding any illegal activities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: CGS International starts YTL Hospitality REIT coverage with RM1.18 target price
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: CGS International has initiated coverage on YTL Hospitality REIT (YTL REIT) with an "Add" call and a dividend discount model-based target price of RM1.18, citing its defensive income profile, earnings visibility and potential distribution per unit (DPU) growth.
Analyst Lucas Tan said the target price, based on a cost of equity of 9.1 per cent, a terminal growth rate of 1.0 per cent and a 2.1 per cent discount to fair value, implies a financial year 2027 (FY27) forecast dividend yield of 7.8 per cent.
This represents a 4.3 percentage point yield spread against the 10-year Malaysian Government Securities (MGS), which is about one standard deviation above its 10-year average.
"We anticipate the yield spread to narrow further given the REIT's strengthening earnings profile, underpinned by visible master lease income, scheduled rental escalations, and incremental contributions from new assets," Tan said in a note.
He said, given this, coupled with an improving tourism outlook driven by Visit Malaysia Year 2026 (VMY2026) and a resilient balance sheet, he views YTL REIT as offering a combination of defensive cash flows and visible FY26-28 DPU growth, warranting a re-rating relative to historical valuations.
The research house said investors have underestimated YTL REIT's portfolio of premium hospitality assets located in key tourism and business destinations across Malaysia, Australia and Japan.
"This makes YTL REIT a prime beneficiary of multiple demand drivers, including international tourism; corporate travel; meetings, incentives, conventions, and exhibition activities; and domestic leisure spending, in our view. Despite that, YTL Reit continues to trade at what we view as undemanding valuations," he said.
He said investor caution has largely centred around two concerns. The first is the REIT's reliance on master lease assets, which contributed about 60 per cent of FY25 net property income (NPI). While these leases provide earnings stability and downside protection, investors may perceive them as offering slower organic growth compared with hospitality REITs with greater exposure to variable hotel income.
The second concern relates to earnings volatility from its Australian portfolio, where income is more directly affected by occupancy rates, room rates, operating costs and foreign exchange movements.
However, he believes these concerns overlook YTL REIT's improving growth outlook, supported by contractual rental increases, asset expansion initiatives and potential acquisitions from the wider YTL Corp Bhd ecosystem.
The research house expects YTL REIT's DPU growth between FY26 and FY28 to be driven by three key factors – periodic rental step-ups under existing master leases, organic growth from asset enhancement initiatives and potential yield-accretive acquisitions and developments.
Several master leases are scheduled for rental revisions in November 2026, including The Ritz-Carlton Kuala Lumpur – Hotel Wing, AC Hotel Kuala Lumpur Titiwangsa, AC Hotel Penang Bukit Jambul, Pangkor Laut Resort, Tanjong Jara Resort, AC Hotel Kuantan City Centre, Cameron Highlands Resort and Hilton Niseko Village.
CGS International estimates YTL REIT's master leases could achieve average rental reversions of about 5 per cent every five years, in line with its historical track record. These revisions could generate additional annual NPI of about RM7.5 million, equivalent to around 2.5 per cent of its FY26 NPI forecast.
The research house also highlighted the recovery of RM75.9 million in deferred rental income between FY26 and FY29, which provides additional earnings visibility.
The deferred rentals arose from a Covid-19 support arrangement in July 2020, when YTL REIT agreed to defer 50 per cent of rental payments for 24 months across its Malaysian hotel portfolio and Hilton Niseko Village. Repayment is scheduled over seven years from July 2022 to June 2029.
Tan said management expects the remaining deferred rental balance to contribute RM19 million, RM43.9 million and RM6.5 million to FY26, FY27 and FY28 earnings, respectively.
Beyond rental growth, he expects new developments to provide further earnings support, including AC Hotel Puchong and Moxy Niseko in Japan.
AC Hotel Puchong commenced its master lease on April 1, 2026, with a fixed annual rental of RM3.64 million, while the RM199 million Moxy Niseko project in Hokkaido is expected to be completed by December 2026.
The research house estimates Moxy Niseko could contribute about RM14 million in annual NPI upon commencement, benefiting from Japan's recovering inbound tourism sector and Niseko's growing appeal as a global ski destination.
Together, AC Hotel Puchong and Moxy Niseko are expected to contribute RM17 million to RM18 million in additional annual NPI, equivalent to about 6 per cent of CGS International's FY26 NPI forecast.
Tan said YTL REIT stands out as an underappreciated high-yield, low-beta play that combines defensive income characteristics with a visible DPU growth trajectory.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Four Melaka DAP assemblymen moved to opposition bloc in state assembly
Article:
MELAKA: The seats of four Melaka DAP assemblymen in the State Legislative Assembly were moved to the opposition bloc on the final day of the sitting today.
The four are now seated alongside Perikatan Nasional and Independent assemblymen after announcing yesterday that they were withdrawing support for the Barisan Nasional (BN)-led government.
They are Allex Seah Shoo Chin (PH-Kesidang), Low Chee Leong (PH-Kota Laksamana), Leng Chau Yen (PH-Banda Hilir) and Kerk Chee Yee (PH-Ayer Keroh).
However, the sole Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) assemblyman, Adly Zahari (PH-Bukit Katil), who is also Melaka Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, remained seated with the government bloc, separate from the four DAP assemblymen.
State Assembly Speaker Datuk Ibrahim Durum expressed hope that all assemblymen would participate in today's sitting with discipline, decorum and courtesy while complying with the House rules.
Yesterday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim urged Melaka DAP to
postpone its decision
so that the focus could remain on development and the people's welfare.
Melaka DAP announced
its immediate withdrawal
from the government after the State Legislative Assembly passed amendments to the State Constitution to allow for the appointment of nominated assemblymen.
Melaka DAP chairman Khoo Poay Tiong said the decision was in line with the party's stand against the bill, which it claimed was contrary to the principles of democracy and elections.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man killed, son critically injured after truck overtakes on double line
Article:
BENTONG: A man was killed while his six-year-old son was seriously injured after their motorcycle collided with a four-wheel-drive vehicle at Km15 of Jalan Utama Mempaga yesterday.
Zaiful Arimey Zainal Abidin, 33, who was riding a Yamaha motorcycle, died at the scene in the 10.45pm accident.
Bentong police chief, Superintendent Zaiham Mohd Kahar, said the accident involved a Toyota Hilux driven by a 43-year-old man and Zaiful's motorcycle.
"The Hilux was travelling from Kuala Lumpur towards Raub, and the driver is believed to have overtaken multiple vehicles across a double line. The vehicle entered the opposite lane before crashing into the oncoming motorcycle.
"Zaiful suffered severe head injuries, along with injuries to his hands and legs, and died at the scene. His son, who was riding pillion, sustained serious injuries to his left leg and is receiving treatment at Bentong Hospital," he said in a statement today.
Zaiham said the Hilux driver and his passenger escaped unhurt.
He added that the driver's urine screening tested negative for drugs and that he would be remanded to facilitate further investigations.
The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for causing death by reckless or dangerous driving.
Upon conviction, the offence carries a prison sentence of between five and 10 years, a fine of between RM20,000 and RM50,000, and disqualification from holding or obtaining a driving licence for at least five years.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 10 commercial vehicle drivers test positive for drugs in  Pahang
Article:
TEMERLOH: Ten of the 351 commercial vehicle drivers screened by the Pahang Road Transport Department (RTD) during its Integrated Anti-Drug Operation at 10 strategic locations in the state since July 1 have tested positive for drugs.
Pahang RTD director Syed Ahmad Khirulanwar Alyahya Syed Abdul Rahman said the operation, now into its 14th day, focused on drivers of commercial, public service and tourism vehicles.
He said that it was carried out in collaboration with the National Anti-Drugs Agency (Nada), police, Land Public Transport Agency (APAD), the Computerised Vehicle Inspection Centres  (Puspakom), highway concessionaires and the Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry.
"So far, the 10 drug positive cases detected involved heavy vehicle drivers aged between 29 and 55.
"Action against them was taken according to the jurisdiction of Nada, while RTD has suspended their Goods Driving Licences (GDLs) pending investigations before further action is taken under the relevant laws," he told a media conference at the Temerloh Rest and Service (R&R) areas here yesterday.
He said that of the 10 operation locations, five cases were detected at the Temerloh R&R, with two each at the Benta Enforcement Station (SPK) and Paloh Hinai SPK, as well as one at the Maran SPK.
He added that most of the drug abuse cases among drivers involved were methamphetamine and the main factor in taking it was suspected to be to boost energy levels and maintain focus while driving.
"These findings prove the need for continuous enforcement operations to ensure that only drivers who are actually qualified, possess integrity and are free from drug abuse are allowed to operate commercial vehicles on public roads," he said.
He said checks also revealed that one of the drivers who tested positive for drugs had a record of 42 RTD summonses, all of which had been settled, while another had 11 summonses and several others had between one and nine summonses.
He added that the records showed that some drivers are repeat offenders, which is a matter of concern for the authorities even though all the summonses have been settled.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia not committed to any nuclear partner yet, says deputy minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia remains at the study stage on nuclear energy and has not committed to any partner country despite reports suggesting otherwise, Deputy Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Mohamad said.
Rahman said Malaysia was engaging with multiple countries that had expressed interest in its nuclear programme, including France, Russia and China, but said that no decisions had been made.
"I was actually quite surprised when a meeting with France two days ago was reported in our newspapers in a way that suggested we had expressed interest in them, when in fact we had not.
"We are still at the study stage, engaging with those who are more experienced in the matter," he said during Minister's Question Time in the Dewan Rakyat.
Rahman was responding to a supplementary question from Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man (PN-Kubang Kerian), who raised China's 2025 breakthrough in the successful conversion of thorium into uranium-233.
Tuan Ibrahim pointed out that major economies, including India, China and the United States, were heavily invested in thorium research and questioned whether Malaysia risked being left behind.
The deputy minister said the government recognised that nuclear energy played an important role in the energy mix and wanted to ensure the right approach was taken.
However, any decision on a nuclear power plant would only be considered after all prerequisites were met and assessments confirmed that the technology was safe, viable and beneficial to the country, he added.
Rahman said the Malaysia Programme Office for Power Electricity Reform (MyPower Corp) had been designated as the nuclear energy programme implementing organisation and was currently conducting a comprehensive, phased assessment of nuclear energy's potential.
On thorium specifically, he said the government was still assessing its options through engagements with more experienced countries, without elaborating on the extent of any thorium-related research being carried out domestically.
Abdul Rahman said the government was committed to accelerating the country's energy transition towards a cleaner, more resilient and sustainable system while preserving what he described as the three pillars of energy policy — supply security, cost affordability and environmental sustainability.
In the near term, he said strategic priority would be given to proven and commercially viable renewable sources, including solar photovoltaic, tri-generation, biogas and hydropower.
On July 9, French Minister Delegate for Foreign Trade and Economic Attractiveness Nicolas Forissier said France was ready to support Malaysia's civil nuclear
energy
ambitions if it decided to include nuclear power in its long-term energy mix.
The decision, however, rests entirely with the Malaysian government, which is still evaluating the option as part of a broader national discussion on the country's future energy mix, he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Six foreigners fined RM1,200 each for conducting World Cup betting activities [WATCH]
Article:
BALIK PULAU: Six foreigners were fined RM1,200 each by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to jointly conducting betting activities on 2026 Fifa World Cup football matches.
The accused were Chinese nationals Yang Zhilong, Zhou Bao, Xu Kai and Ma Jiahao, and Myanmar nationals Khaing Moe Nwe and Le Kyar Hkyan, aged between 23 and 31.
The Chinese nationals are all men, while the Myanmar nationals are women.
Magistrate Chia Huey Ting imposed the fine on each accused, with one month's imprisonment in default of payment.
According to the charge, they jointly accepted bets on 2026 Fifa World Cup football matches at a condominium in Persiaran Mahsuri, Sungai Nibong Kecil, Bayan Baru, at 6.45pm on July 1.
They were charged under Section 6(1) of the Betting Act 1953, read together with Section 34 of the Penal Code, which provides for a fine of up to RM5,000, jail of up to six months, or both, upon conviction.
Based on the facts of the case, the accused were believed to be part of a syndicate conducting 2026 Fifa World Cup betting activities targeting customers from China.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Ikmal Affandi Zulkifli prosecuted, while all six accused were unrepresented.
Earlier, the accused indicated they understood the charge and the facts of the case after they were read to them in Mandarin by a court interpreter before entering their guilty pleas.
In mitigation, they asked for a lenient sentence.
Ikmal urged the court to impose an appropriate punishment.
"This is to serve as a lesson to the accused and others," he told the court.
Separately, five of the accused, except Le Kyar, pleaded guilty to a separate charge under Section 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for failing to produce valid travel documents to enter Malaysia lawfully.
Ikmal told the court there were no immigration records showing they had entered the country.
Chia fined Ma and Khaing RM3,300 each after they told the court they had been in Malaysia for almost a month.
She also fined Yang, Zhou and Xu RM3,000 each after they admitted they had been in the country for about 20 days.
"We entered the country together, taking a flight to Kuala Lumpur before travelling by land to Penang," Yang told the court.
The magistrate ordered them to serve one month's jail in default of payment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China's economic growth hits slowest pace in more than three years
Article:
BEIJING: China's economy grew at its weakest pace in more than three years during the second quarter, data showed Wednesday, missing expectations even as strong exports driven by the global AI boom helped offset trade disruptions caused by the Middle East war.
The 4.3 per cent year-on-year expansion reported by the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) for April-June, was short of the 4.5 per cent forecast in an AFP survey of economists and the slowest growth since the fourth quarter of 2022.
It was also short of the 4.5-5.0 per cent annual rate targeted by Beijing, which is the lowest in decades.
A years-long crisis in the property sector and a persistent slump in domestic spending have left leaders reliant on exports to meet growth targets.
However, the US-Israeli war on Iran has threatened that as it chokes shipping through the Strait of Hormuz – a vital transit route through which a fifth of global oil and natural gas normally passes.
"In the first half of the year, the national economy operated within a reasonable range," the NBS said in a statement.
"There are many unstable and uncertain external factors, and the domestic contradiction of strong supply and weak demand is prominent. The foundation for the economy to improve still needs to be consolidated," it added.
The NBS data also showed retail sales grew 1.0 year-on-year in June, beating a Bloomberg forecast of a 0.1 per cent drop.
And industrial production rose 5.3 per cent last month, topping a Bloomberg estimate of 4.6 per cent.
But in a gloomy sign, fixed-asset investment slid 5.7 percent on-year in the first half.
Domestic demand dampened by low income expectations remains China's "weakest link", Yue Su of The Economist Intelligence Unit told AFP.
"We therefore expect policymakers to place greater emphasis on boosting consumption in the second half of the year and into early 2027" through fiscal stimulus packages, increased minimum wages or directing wage growth towards frontline workers, she said.
But analyst Zhang Zhiwei said the government was unlikely to change its policy stance in the coming months as a result of the latest figures.
"We need to keep in mind that the first-quarter GDP growth was strong at five per cent. This means the government is still on track to deliver growth in line with the official (annual) target they set at 4.5-5 per cent," Zhang wrote in a note.
"The export boom just continues to beat expectations, and it will likely remain strong in the short term," he added.
The closely watched figures followed data Tuesday that showed exports surged a forecast-topping 27.0 per cent year-on-year in June as the global AI boom helped fuel demand for chips and computing equipment.
China's semiconductor exports more than doubled in value in June year-on-year, while data-processing equipment shipments rose 53.1 percent from a year earlier.
But that expansion was "entirely a price story caused by the ongoing shortage of memory chips", Julian Evans-Pritchard of Capital Economics said Tuesday, noting that the volume of semiconductor exports actually fell year-on-year in June.
Tensions with the United States and European Union remain a source of friction.
China remains locked in a simmering trade feud with the EU, with which it recorded a trade surplus of US$32.9 billion in June.
And while ties between Washington and Beijing have stabilised since US President Donald Trump visited Beijing in May, a trade imbalance and rivalry over chip production persist.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Israel nationals found in Malaysia will be deported immediately
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — Malaysia will not compromise and will deport any Israeli nationals found entering the country, as it has a clear policy of not recognising Israel, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
The Prime Minister said all relevant security agencies were conducting a thorough investigation into allegations that Israeli nationals had been detected in Malaysia.
“We are investigating... we will not allow it. If there are any, action must be taken. If there are Israeli nationals, since we do not recognise Israel, they will be deported immediately,” he told reporters here today.
He was commenting on media reports alleging that Israeli nationals using dual citizenship documents had been found in Johor.
Elaborating, Anwar said the matter was being handled by the authorities and expressed confidence that the relevant ministry would provide further details.
“All the agencies are carrying out investigations. I believe Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir will provide an explanation,” he said.
Yesterday, the Johor government urged the Home Ministry and relevant agencies to investigate the operation of the Network School in Forest City, which was alleged to involve Israeli nationals.
Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the individuals were believed to have used passports from a second country to enter Malaysia to participate in the programme, raising public concern.
Earlier, allegations circulated on social media claiming that foreign nationals possessing Israeli passports as well as citizenship documents from other countries had attempted to enter or were present in Malaysia for certain purposes. — Bernama

Title: No need to review Budget 2027 projections despite higher fuel subsidy bill, says deputy minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — There is no need to review current projections under Budget 2027 based on the government’s fiscal performance for the first half of 2026, said Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong.
However, he said the government would continue to monitor current developments until Budget 2027 is tabled in October.
He said higher oil prices had increased government subsidy expenditure by an estimated RM40 billion.
“At the same time, this has also contributed to higher petroleum-related revenue, where every US$1 per barrel increase in world crude oil prices would likely add about RM300 million to petroleum-related revenue.
“This is excluding Petronas’ dividend contribution. The revenue gain can help cover part of the increase in fuel subsidy expenditure,” he said during the Oral Answers session in the Dewan Rakyat today.
Liew was replying to a supplementary question from Mohd Syahir Che Sulaiman (PN-Bachok) regarding the impact of increased fuel subsidies following the West Asia conflict on the Budget 2027 framework currently being drafted, the 2026 fiscal deficit target and the possibility of new taxes due to strains on the country’s fiscal position.
He said the government also regularly monitors revenue collection performance to ensure it can meet its operating expenditure needs amid geopolitical developments in West Asia.
“We are also monitoring the current economic situation through the implementation of continuous weekly engagement sessions by the crisis management task force under the National Economic Action Council (NEAC) to ensure that energy supplies and basic needs remain protected,” he said.
Commenting on the 2026 fiscal projections, Liew said the government has implemented immediate, comprehensive, and structured intervention initiatives to ensure that overall government expenditure could be maintained without affecting the country’s fiscal position.
“Fiscal projections will be announced during the tabling of Budget 2027 after taking into account current economic indicators as well as the performance of government revenue collection and expenditure in the first half of 2026,” he said.
He said the government remains confident that the medium-term fiscal consolidation trajectory can be maintained through the implementation of targeted subsidies, restructuring expenditure priorities, continued efforts to improve spending efficiency, and bolstering revenue collection and tax compliance.
Liew said the measures would ensure the country’s fiscal position remains sustainable while meeting government spending needs amid global economic uncertainties and current geopolitical developments. — Bernama

Title: Sabah seeks Budi Diesel revamp as state highlights burden on farmers, rural communities
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, July 15 — Sabah expects revisions and improvements to the BUDI targeted diesel subsidy scheme within the next two to three weeks following engagement sessions between the state government and relevant federal ministries.
State Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry Minister Datuk Jamawi Jaafar said the issue was raised at a recent State Cabinet meeting after discussions with the Finance Ministry and the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) on the subsidy scheme’s impact on Sabahans.
“I presented a comprehensive report on how our farmers have been affected, how farm machinery has been impacted, how pickup truck owners have been affected, as well as young people who rely on pickup trucks and jeeps for work and business.
“The Chief Minister took the matter seriously and instructed the State Cabinet to invite the Ministry of Finance and KPDN to conduct briefing and engagement sessions with civil servants and government elected representatives,” he said.
